web.title: User picker package - Atlassian Frontend Development - Atlaskit
web.description: This is the platform field for selecting users. On top of that you can also select Teams. This package provides two different modes of selection.

# User picker

[Examples](https://atlaskit.atlassian.com/packages/elements/user-picker/example/single)

Fabric component for display a dropdown to select a user from

Install

`yarn add @atlaskit/user-picker`

npm

[@atlaskit/user-picker](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@atlaskit/user-picker)

Source

[Bitbucket](https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-frontend-mirror/src/master/elements/user-picker)

Bundle

[unpkg.com](https://unpkg.com/@atlaskit/user-picker/dist/)

[Changelog](https://atlaskit.atlassian.com/packages/elements/user-picker/changelog)

Latest

## 11.25.5

### Patch Changes

- [`608c375f6f9b9`](https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-frontend-monorepo/commits/608c375f6f9b9) -
Internal changes to remove unnecessary token fallbacks and imports from `@atlaskit/theme`

* * *

## Note: This component is designed for internal Atlassian development.

External contributors will be able to use this component but will not be able to submit issues.

This is the platform field for selecting users. On top of that you can also select [Teams﻿, (opens new window)](https://developer.atlassian.com/platform/teams/overview/what-are-teams/).
This package provides two different modes of selection: single and multi user/team picker.

#### DISCLAIMER:

Please consider using the smart-user-picker over user-picker if possible. This encourages consistency and monitoring.
If you cannot use smart-user-picker, please reach out to the our slack channels, #search-plex,
so that we can work with you.

## Usage

Import the component in your React app as follows:

`import UserPicker from '@atlaskit/user-picker';`

Single User Picker

Enter people or teams...

Multi User Picker

, multiple selections available,

Enter people or teams...

Watchers

Enter people or teams...

## User Picker Props

### `addMoreMessage``string`

Message to encourage the user to add more items to user picker.

### `allowEmail``boolean`

Whether the user is allowed to enter emails as a value.

### `anchor``React.ComponentType<any>`

Anchor for the user picker popup.

`React.ComponentType<any>`

Appearance of the user picker.

`One of <"normal",
"compact"

Accessibility: Identifies the element (or elements) that describe the current element.

### `ariaLabel``string`

Accessibility: Uses to set aria-label of the input

### `ariaLabelledBy``string`

Accessibility: Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.

### `ariaLive``union`

Accessibility: Used to set the priority with which screen reader should treat updates to live regions.

`One of <"polite",
"off",
"assertive"

>`

### `autoFocus``boolean`

Override the internal behaviour to automatically focus the control when the picker is open

### `captureMenuScroll``boolean`

React-select prop for blocking menu scroll on container when menu scrolled to the very top/bottom of the menu

### `clearValueLabel``string`

Optional tooltip to display on hover over the clear indicator.

### `disableInput``boolean`

Whether to disable interaction with the input

### `emailLabel``string`

Email option label

### `fieldId``union`` required`

- Used to configure additional information regarding where the
- user picker has been mounted.
- The purpose is to give more context as to where user picker events
- are being fired from, as the current data may not uniquely identify
- which field is the source.
- The value will be passed as a data attribute for analytics.
- Examples include "assignee", "watchers" and "share".
- A second usage for the fieldId is for server side rendering (SSR) where it must be a unique id per UserPicker
- instance contained in the serialized SSR content. E.g. a SPA page rendered through SSR that has multiple user pickers.
- fieldId can be set to null if the integrator is not listening
- for the analytic events or does not care about SSR.

`One of <string,
null

Display a remove button on the single picker. True by default.

### `isDisabled``boolean`

Disable all interactions with the picker, putting it in a read-only state.

### `isFooterFocused``boolean`

Checks if the footer is focused or not. This is needed to keep the menu open when the footer is focused

### `isHeaderFocused``boolean`

Checks if the header is focused or not. This is needed to keep the menu open when the header is focused

Display the picker with a style to show the value is invalid

# Searching using a custom single user picker does not display all users

[![Seth Clayton](https://avatar-management--avatars.us-west-2.prod.public.atl-paas.net/initials/SC-2.png)](https://community.atlassian.com/forums/user/viewprofilepage/user-id/1315073)

[Seth Clayton](https://community.atlassian.com/forums/user/viewprofilepage/user-id/1315073)

April 1, 2022 edited

We defined a few custom single user picker fields in Jira (ex. Responsible QA). These fields have been associated with the project, issue types, screens, schemes, etc. and work perfectly fine when you add someone to a ticket. So if I open a ticket, I can see that Paul Smith is listed in the Responsible QA field.

The problem we face is when you go to search and select the custom user picker as a field, only a few users/groups are displayed in the list of options. Typing the name into the field's typeahead yields no matches.

If I select one of the users that does appear, the search works as expected. If I switch to JQL and search for Paul Smith in the Responsible QA field, the search works as expected.

I've checked user settings for people who appear as well as people who do not appear and can't find anything different. For instance, only one admin (me) appears in the list while another admin with the same permissions and groups does not appear.

We're using cloud and have ~400 users (about half are inactive).

Is this a user setting I'm missing or a bug?

I understand that you stated that you have already reviewed the permissions and etc, but some things that if you haven't seen it and you can check it out are something like:

Check the "browse users and groups" global permission if the groups you and the rest of the team participate in are within that permission;

![83.PNG](https://community.atlassian.com/forums/image/serverpage/image-id/191240i2382FB5F9DF78D6F/image-size/large?v=v2&px=999)

Check that the user you want to add to the "QA" field, for example, has a license for the Jira Software or Jira Service Management product (it will depend on your project);

Check that in the custom field, in the "Edit context and default value" option, there is not a user selection filter or a context applied to your project.

If you've already reviewed this and it still doesn't seem to work, can you come back with a few screenshots so we can understand this behavior?

## post by RomanHavryliv on Apr 14, 2022

[![](https://avatars.discourse-cdn.com/v4/letter/r/fbc32d/48.png)](https://community.developer.atlassian.com/u/romanhavryliv)

Hi all!

I have next situation:

created custom watchers field for Jira Cloud using `Jira custom field (beta)`; field type is `user` \+ `collection: list` (as far as we can have a number of users); BUT in **advanced search** we have Input instead of User Picker.

In case we have `type: user` without `collection: list` we have User Picker in advanced search.

[![image](https://global.discourse-cdn.com/atlassiandeveloper/optimized/3X/b/2/b23926d46015c529a9002d980b42867f58bc111a_2_497x500.png)\\
image1016×1022 61.1 KB](https://global.discourse-cdn.com/atlassiandeveloper/original/3X/b/2/b23926d46015c529a9002d980b42867f58bc111a.png "image")

**Question #1:** should be there added smth specific to User Picker in advanced search for `type: user` \+ `collection: list`?

We also think that User Picker for collection is not added yet because of **beta** version of `Jira custom field (beta)`.

At the same time we have another problem. We tried to add searchSuggestions function to show suggestions for Input field (as a workaroud) but inputting any query doesn’t invoke our function (have nothing in console during tunneling).

In `manifest.yml`:

```yaml
  jira:customField:
    - key: custom-watchers-field-app-hello-world
      name: Watchers Custom
      description: A custom watchers field.
      type: user
      collection: list
      readOnly: false
      function: main
      edit:
        function: edit
      searchSuggestions:
        function: search
  function:
    - key: main
      handler: index.runView
```

Hello, [@RomanHavryliv](https://community.developer.atlassian.com/u/romanhavryliv),\
\
I think you mean _basic_ search? Advanced search is when the user inputs raw JQL queries, basic search is the one where they can use the UI to select values.\
\
Anyway, user list fields should show the user picker, just like singular user fields. It’s a bug that they currently don’t. I’m adding this to our backlog. Will let you know here when it’s fixed.\
\
As for the search suggestions function, it only affects the values that are suggested in the advanced search, it doesn’t control the basic search experience.\

# Virtual Agent Intents - User Picker (multiple users) custom field support

[![David O'Dowd](https://avatar-management--avatars.us-west-2.prod.public.atl-paas.net/initials/DO-4.png)](https://community.atlassian.com/forums/user/viewprofilepage/user-id/5328533)

[David O'Dowd](https://community.atlassian.com/forums/user/viewprofilepage/user-id/5328533)

June 10, 2024 edited

Hi All,

We're starting to take advantage of automation wherever and whenever we can, and have built several automations based on webhooks sent from Jira Service Management.

We are also attempting to integrate these into the Virtual Agent, but have hit a stumbling block.

In several forms we have a custom field that is a User Picker (multiple users) custom field which works perfectly when submitting help desk tickets through the user portal.

However, when attempting to integrate this form into the Virtual Agent it states that the field type is not yet supported.

Is there any ETA on when this type of field is going to be integrated into the Virtual Agent? It would be massively beneficial for any intents where the Agent is trying to collect multiple users to be added to, or removed from, any number of things.

June 22, 2024 edited

Thanks for your question about integrating custom fields, specifically the User Picker (multiple users) field, into the Virtual Agent for Jira Service Management.

Currently, the Atlassian Virtual Agent does not support the User Picker (multiple users) custom field. This limitation can be a challenge when trying to collect multiple users in forms via the Virtual Agent. Atlassian is aware of this need, and while there isn't a specific ETA for when this feature will be integrated, it is on the radar for future updates: [JSM Questions](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-Service-Management/User-picker-field-behaves-differently-for-customer-than-for/qaq-p/2554391) and [JSM Questions 2](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-Service-Management/How-does-the-User-Picker-work-for-Jira-Service-Desk-approvals/qaq-p/1174946)

For now, you might consider alternative ways to gather user information, such as using text fields where users can list names or emails, which can then be processed manually or through subsequent automation steps.

For the latest updates on feature requests and developments, you can keep an eye on the Jira Cloud Public Issue Tracker. This tracker will have information on the status of upcoming features and enhancements.
